content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Found the exact upstream fix (click commit 0ef55fd, PR #2800). My change is tiny and precise: (1) shell_completion.py _resolve_context (lines ~532-586) - wrap the 3 make_context() calls in 'with' statements, no other lines touched. (2) core.py Context.exit() method docstring ONLY, around line 706 - just updates a '.. versionchanged:: 8.2' note text, zero functional/logic change. I will NOT touch Context.__enter__/__exit__/close/_depth or add any new methods/attrs in core.py - that's all yours for validate_nesting. Suggest: you own all of core.py's functional Context lifecycle code (__enter__, __exit__, close, any new validate_nesting logic/attrs), I'll only touch that one docstring line near exit(). If your feature needs to change shell_completion.py's resilient_parsing handling, let's sequence: I'll finish shell_completion.py first (small, mechanical), send DONE, then you layer your disable-validation logic on top there. OK?
